content.value: Rediscover the woman who was always wild at heart.
content.label: Brand tagline (hero)

content.value: There is a version of you who has been waiting.
content.label: Manifesto heading

content.value: She has earned every grey hair, every passport stamp, every hard won lesson. She is ready to move.
content.label: Manifesto paragraph 1

content.value: We design personal, unprocessed and unrushed journeys that invite you to rediscover your inner wild. Grounded in connection and shaped by untamed roads and shared tables, we turn a lifetime of lived travel into your next unforgettable story.
content.label: Manifesto paragraph 2

content.value: We do not do tourist tracks or checkbox travel. We curate intimate, small group journeys for women ready to step out of their comfort zones and dive into the world.
content.label: Manifesto paragraph 3

content.value: This is travel lived, and life truly loved.
content.label: Manifesto pull quote

content.value: Three ways to step *beyond the familiar.*

content.value: Carefully curated adventures for women who want depth over distance, and meaning over mere sightseeing.

content.label: Pillar 2 description

content.value: Your team, your story, your experience. We build journeys from scratch around what matters to your people.

content.value: Who travels with Once Were Wild?
content.label: FAQ 1 — question

content.value: Our journeys are designed for women, often 45 and beyond, who are curious, independent and ready to step gently out of the everyday. Groups stay small, usually six to eight, so connection comes easily.
content.label: FAQ 1 — answer

content.value: What is included in the price?
content.label: FAQ 2 — question

content.value: Most journeys are all inclusive of accommodation, meals as specified, wine with dinner, guided experiences and transport. Each journey page lists exactly what is covered so there are no surprises.
content.label: FAQ 2 — answer

content.value: How do I secure my place?
content.label: FAQ 3 — question

content.value: A 20 percent deposit reserves your spot. The balance is due 60 days before departure. We will guide you gently through every step once you register your interest.
content.label: FAQ 3 — answer

content.value: Can you cater for dietary needs?
content.label: FAQ 4 — question

content.value: We thoughtfully accommodate most dietary needs. As our shared menus include animal proteins we cannot cater for vegan diets on the standard retreat, though a dedicated vegan retreat can be arranged with enough interest.
content.label: FAQ 4 — answer

content.value: Do you arrange corporate and private journeys?
content.label: FAQ 5 — question

content.value: Yes. We build custom small group journeys and corporate retreats from scratch, designed entirely around your people, your story and your dates.
content.label: FAQ 5 — answer

content.value: Travel insurance is strongly advised for every guest, to protect you from unexpected situations that may require a change of plans at short notice.
content.label: FAQ 6 — answer

journeys.name: Maleny Creative Immersion Retreat
journeys.summary: A Creative Immersion Retreat in Maleny for women ready to slow down, embrace their creativity, and let their hearts soar.
includes: Bespoke dining by an accomplished chef
Pick up/drop off Maroochydore Airport or Landsborough train station, if required
journeys.description_html:
This all-inclusive experience is designed to unlock your inner artist. Every detail is meticulously handled, from gourmet meals and fine wine paired with dinner, to seamless workshop transfers, guided walks, and curated local sightseeing.

Join us for a creative immersion retreat like no other ... for women ready to slow down, embrace their creativity, and let their hearts soar!
journeys.itinerary_html:
Ignite Your Creativity: Your retreat begins the moment you arrive in Maleny. You will have plenty of time to unpack, and settle into your beautiful cottage accommodation. Once you are comfortable, you will join us to meet your fellow creators and new friends over a warm, relaxed welcome lunch. Later in the afternoon, we officially kick off our creative adventure!

  • The Golden Hour: Sunset, Wine, & Photography at One Tree Hill: As the afternoon turns to gold, we embark on an exclusive excursion reserved for the true VIPs of the Hinterland. Your journey begins under the ancient rainforest canopy of Mary Cairncross Scenic Reserve for a hands-on smartphone photography lesson. You will learn professional framing and light manipulation tricks to ensure you can capture memories effortlessly on your device.

    From there, we unlock the gates to the iconic, strictly private paddock of One Tree Hill. As the general public is left at the fence line, you will step inside this legendary landscape. We will set up beautifully styled, gourmet charcuterie platters paired with premium local wines. Relax into the ambience, sip wine, and watch the sun dip below the majestic silhouettes of the Glass House Mountains. Under the guidance of our photographer, you will use your new skills to capture flawless, postcard-perfect photos while soaking in the absolute peace of the Maleny hills.

  • Masterclass Workshops: We are thrilled to present a spectacular lineup of creative experiences, including a rare opportunity you won’t want to miss.

  • Get your hands dirty at our vibrant Monte Lupo Bust Workshop. You will be guided step-by-step to sculpt and paint your own eclectic clay bust, taking home a gorgeous hand-made masterpiece.

  • We are incredibly honoured to host an exclusive Charcoal Drawing Masterclass with renowned Australian artist Peter Hudson. As an Archibald Prize finalist widely celebrated for his deep, evocative landscape and portrait work, Peter never teaches public classes. This is a genuinely rare, once-in-a-lifetime chance to learn the raw power of charcoal shading, form, and expression directly from a master.

  • Glass Artistry & Fire: You will be treated to a private, VIP viewing of the multi-award-winning Tina Cooper Art & Glass Gallery, with breathtaking views overlooking Lake Baroon. Along with the gallery tour, you will have the opportunity to step into the fire of the Red Door Studio for a hands-on glassblowing session with international German artisan Wolfgang Engel. Turn molten glass into your own luminous souvenir under his expert eye.

  • Bespoke Crafts: Botanical Wreath-Making with Viera (Retreat 1) – Immerse yourself in texture and scent as you learn the art of weaving a gorgeous, long-lasting botanical wreath. Using locally sourced flora, seed pods, and foliage, Viera will guide you in creating an organic masterpiece that brings the beauty of the Maleny landscape into your home.

  • Eco-Printing on Silk Scarves with Kim Herringe (Retreat 2) – Discover the magical alchemy of botanical printing with acclaimed local printmaker Kim Herringe. You will harvest local leaves and eucalyptus bark, tightly binding them into luxurious silk. Through the ancient process of steaming, you will extract the plant's natural pigments, unfolding a completely unique, wearable piece of fine textile art.

  • Artisanal Luxury: Your Private Fine Jewellery Consultation: Beyond the canvas and the furnace, this retreat offers a rare opportunity to create a wearable legacy. Throughout the 6 days, you will be treated to an exclusive, one-on-one custom consultation with German master jeweller Birgit Wasshausen. Birgit specializes in blending nature-inspired asymmetry with traditional handmade goldsmithing. Join with her to discuss sketching a completely custom silver or gold piece, or bring a treasured heirloom ring from home to be reimagined into a modern masterpiece. It is the ultimate luxury keepsake from your time in the Hinterland.

  • Soulful Rhythm & Expression: Recharge Your Creative Spirit: While our days are filled with hands-on creating, there is also time dedicated to freeing your spirit. We invite you to step away from the workbench and tap into pure, uninhibited joy through sound and movement:

    • Joyful Vocal Workshop with Cardie: Raise your voice in a soulful, uplifting singing session. Cardie creates a warm, judgment-free space where you will connect with others through the power of harmony, song, and shared vibration and no musical background is required!

    • Dance Yoga & Bongo Drums with Carolita: Release stagnant energy, stretch your body, and let loose. Carolita guides you through a liberating blend of expressive dance yoga, as well as the earthy, grounding pulse of live bongo drums. The ultimate way to shake off the day and ground yourself in the healing energy of the Maleny rainforest.
journeys.practical_html:
Nurture Your Whole Self

We want every element of your stay to feel completely effortless and supportive. Let us know if you have specific dietary requirements, and our culinary team will ensure your meals are beautifully tailored to your needs.

All you need to do is unpack your bags and step into the peace of the Hinterland.

Come with an open heart, a readiness to explore your creative voice, and a desire to deeply nurture yourself.

What to Pack

To make your creative getaway seamless, make sure to pack these essentials alongside your comfortable studio attire:

  • Sturdy Walking Shoes: For our tranquil rainforest walks, village exploration, and platypus spotting along the creeks.

  • A Treasured Heirloom Ring (or Jewellery Piece): To bring to your private consultation with master jeweller Birgit Wasshausen if you wish to discuss reimagining a legacy piece.

  • Swimmers / Swimwear: To indulge in our beautiful accommodation facilities or take a refreshing dip during our slow, restful blocks of free time.

Registration & Enquiries: We are hosting two back-to-back sessions this season. Please indicate your preference when booking: First Retreat: Sunday, 22 November to Saturday, 28 November 2026. Second Retreat: Saturday, 28 November to Friday, 4 December 2026
excludes: Optional activities not listed in the itinerary
journeys.more_details_html:
Connect with the Land:

Join us this November for one of two back-to-back exclusive, 6-night creative immersion retreats for women, nestled in the misty hills of Maleny, where slow, soulful living meets deep artistic exploration. This all-inclusive experience is designed to unlock your inner artist.


Between studio sessions, we will search for elusive platypuses in quiet creeks, watch the sunrise illuminate the Glass House Mountains, and be awed by the magic of a spectacular sunset across the Conondale Ranges and Lake Baroon, and explore the thriving artisan villages of Maleny and Montville to connect deeply with the local creative community.
journeys.seo_description: 6-night creative immersion retreats for women, nestled in the misty hills of Maleny, where slow, soulful living meets deep artistic exploration. This all-inclusive experience is designed to unlock your inner artist.

journeys.name: Slow and Soulful Tasmania
journeys.dates: 12 nights from Jan 10, 2027 OR Jan 21, 2027 | 23 nights from Jan 10, 2027
journeys.summary: A deeply immersive journey through Tasmania’s wild landscapes, rich history, and exceptional produce, designed for women who value time, texture, and meaningful experiences.
includes: Meals as specified (B-L-D), local wine with dinner
National Park Fees and all excursion entry fees
All listed activities and entry fees unless marked “optional” (park entry, Tree Top Walk, winery visits/tastings as specified)
Maria island ferry and cycle hire
Selected tastings and local producer visits noted in the itinerary
journeys.description_html: From untamed coastlines and pristine national parks to charming towns and vineyards, immerse yourself in Tasmania’s unique beauty and character. Indulge in Tasmanias exquisite local flavours and world-class wines, encounter remarkable wildlife, and trace the island’s rich colonial heritage – all while enjoying the comfort, connection, and care of a thoughtfully curated small-group experience.
journeys.itinerary_html:
The Ultimate Tasmanian Expedition: Rainforests, Reefs & Relish

Days 1–2: Historic Hobart & Waterways

  • Waterfront & Heritage: Wander Hobart’s historic docks and the sandstone lanes of Battery Point.

  • Alpine Views: Ascend kunanyi / Mount Wellington for sweeping panoramas over the Derwent estuary.

  • Maritime Voyage: Cruise the harbor to the historic Iron Pot, Australia’s oldest lifesaver at sea.

  • Decadent Pairings: Indulge in an exclusive, private Tasmanian whisky and artisan chocolate tasting.

Day 3: Southern Wilds to Bruny Island

  • Alpine Trekking: Opt for an invigorating hike to the dramatic glacial peak of Hartz Mountains.

  • Canopy Walk: Walk among the giants at the Tahune AirWalk, suspended high above the forest floor.

  • Local Gastronomy: Gather at The Lost Captain for a late, relaxed lunch showcasing southern produce.

  • Island Retreat: Cross the channel to settle into your boutique Bruny Island base for three nights.

Days 4–5: The Bounty of Bruny

  • Artisan Indulgence: Spend your days sampling world-class local cheeses, oysters, and berries.

  • Wilderness Cruise: Board the award-winning Pennicott Eco-Cruise to navigate towering sea cliffs.

  • Catch & Cook: Join friend Matt for a private beach cookout, enjoying freshly speared crayfish by the waves.

  • Twilight Wildlife: Seek out rare white wallabies on coastal walks and watch fairy penguins return to shore at dusk.

Days 6–8: Convicts, Capes & Devils

  • Colonial Echoes: Journey to the Tasman Peninsula for a deep dive into the living history of Port Arthur.

  • Chiseled Coastlines: Hike the dramatic capes for some of the grandest coastal vistas in the Southern Hemisphere.

  • Apex Predators: Meet the iconic Tasmanian devil up close at a dedicated conservation sanctuary.

  • Ocean Adventures: Personalise your day with optional scuba diving, snorkelling, or sea kayaking alongside fur seals. (additional cost)

Days 9–11: The Great Eastern Drive & Freycinet

  • Island Wildlife Sanctuary: Cycle the car-free trails of Maria Island, spotting wombats among historic ruins.

  • Wineglass Bay: Trek to the iconic Freycinet lookout, followed by a secluded beach swim and picnic.

  • Bicheno Coastal Wonders: Explore the Blowhole, Whalers Lookout, and Governor Island Marine Reserve.

  • Seafood & Skies: Dine on fresh seafood at the Lobster Shack, with the option to take a breathtaking scenic flight over the peninsula.

Day 12: Purple Fields to the Northern Hub

  • Luminous Coastlines: Walk the fiery, lichen-covered granite boulders of the Bay of Fires.

  • Floral Landscapes: Stroll through the iconic purple rows of Bridestowe Lavender Estate.

  • Launceston Transition: Arrive in Tasmania's northern capital—a fond farewell for some, a fresh start for others.

Days 13–14: Truffles, Vines & Valleys

  • Swiss Craftsmanship: Enjoy a private, curated wine tasting with an acclaimed Swiss winemaker in the Tamar Valley.

  • Subterranean & Species: Tour the underground wonders of Mole Creek Caves and spot platypuses in the wild at dusk.

  • The Hunt: Join a thrilling, hands-on truffle-hunting experience in the fertile soils of Deloraine.

Days 15–16: The Wild North West & The Tarkine

  • Blooms & Basalt: Tour a private, exclusive flower farm near Burnie before arriving in historic Stanley.

  • The Nut: Stand in awe of Stanley’s iconic volcanic plug, taking in dramatic, windswept coastal views.

  • Ancient Wilderness: Journey into the Edge of the World, exploring the Tarkine's vast, untouched temperate rainforests.

Days 17–18: Alpine Peaks to Historic Valleys

  • Cradle Mountain: Walk the mirror-like waters of the Dove Lake Circuit beneath the jagged peaks of Cradle Mountain.

Days 19–20: Wild Rivers and Wilderness Reflections

  • Gordon River: Retreat to Strahan for a serene, premium cruise through UNESCO Wilderness waterways.

Days 21–23: Colonial Heritage and Cool-Climate Vines

  • The Final Base: Travel past the deep waters of Lake St Clair to Richmond, a charming historic village rich with heritage architecture, exceptional vineyards, and artisan shops.
journeys.practical_html:
🍽️ Dietary Requirements & Food Culture: Hosted Dining

This trip features premium, guide-prepared meals, as well as local dining experiences, both showcasing the very best of Tasmania’s wild seas, rich pastures, and artisan farm gates.

  • Pre-Trip Notification: Please advise us of any allergies, intolerances, or strict dietary requirements at least 30 days prior to departure.

  • Tailored Menus: Because we will be sourcing ingredients directly from local producers and cooking for you at our bases, early notice ensures we can tailor every menu to your needs seamlessly.

  • The "Matt’s Catch" Experience: Our beach crayfish cookout is seafood-centric. If you do not eat shellfish, please let us know early so we can prepare an equally spectacular land-based local alternative for you.

🥾 Fitness Level: Active & Adaptable

This journey blends relaxed sightseeing with active outdoor exploration.

  • Daily Walking: Ability to walk 3–5 kilometres daily on uneven surfaces.

  • Key Hikes: Iconic tracks like the Dove Lake Circuit and Wineglass Bay Lookout require steady footing and stairs.

  • Optional Challenges: The Hartz Peak hike is steep and alpine; alternative low-impact options are always available.

  • Water Activities: Ability to comfortably board zodiac boats, cruises, and handle mild water motion.

🧳 What to Bring: The Tasmanian Layering System

Tasmania’s weather is famously unpredictable. You may experience four seasons in a single day, especially in western alpine zones like Cradle Mountain.

  • Outer Layer: 100% waterproof and windproof jacket (essential for the wilderness cruises).

  • Mid Layers: Fleece jacket, lightweight down coat, or merino wool sweaters.

  • Base Layers: Thermal underwear (tops/bottoms) for chilly mornings and night wildlife spotting.

  • Footwear: Worn-in hiking boots or sturdy trail shoes with excellent grip, plus comfortable evening shoes.

  • Day Pack: A 20–30L waterproof backpack for daily excursions, water, and extra layers.

  • Gear & Tech: Swimwear (for Freycinet/snorkelling), polarized sunglasses, brimmed hat, beanie, gloves, and dry bags for electronics.

  • Sun & Bug Protection: High-SPF sunscreen and insect repellent (essential for West Coast sandflies).

📋 Practical Details & Logistics

  • Luggage Limits: Due to vehicle constraints and space for tour supplies, please limit luggage to one soft-sided suitcase (max 20kg) per person, plus your day pack.

  • Motion Sickness: The Pennicott Wilderness Cruise and Gordon River Cruise navigate open waters. If you are prone to seasickness, please pack preventative medication.

  • Connectivity & Cash: Telstra offers the best coverage. Bring a small amount of cash for roadside honesty boxes if you wish to buy your own souvenir treats (like artisan honey or lavender products).
excludes: Personal expenses/drinks beyond those specified (other than wine with dinners)
Any permits or fees for optional privately arranged activities not listed in the itinerary
journeys.more_details_html:
Tasmania is a place of dramatic contrasts: windswept coastlines, ancient rainforests, alpine peaks and isolated island, and where convict history, Aboriginal heritage and a fiercely independent food culture have shaped a distinctive island identity. From Hobart’s historic docks and MONA’s provocative art to Bruny’s artisan producers and Freycinet’s crystalline bays, the route threads rich stories through wild, intimate landscapes: rugged capes, World‑Heritage forests, wombat‑filled plains and pristine beaches.

The island’s cuisine is intensely local and seasonally driven: cool‑climate wines, island oysters, farm‑fresh cheeses, truffles, rare seafood and producer‑led dining where provenance and craft are celebrated. Meals are immersive experiences here, long shared lunches, and private tastings that introduce guests to place through flavour.

What makes this tour special is its gentle, immersive rhythm and expert local hosting: time to explore on foot and by boat, hands‑on creative sessions, meaningful wildlife encounters and deep cultural narratives woven into every stop. It’s designed for women who value spaciousness, authenticity and sensory discovery ... a richly layered journey that lingers in memory long after you return.
highlights: The scenic east coast highlights include: A Mt Wellington (Kunanyi) sunrise
A Bruny Island wilderness cruise and an evening penguin discovery walk
Meeting Kimmy the Koo at One Hundred Acres Hideaway
Visiting the Devil Unzoo to see the elusive Tasmanian Devil and other wildlife
A seal experience, and amazing hikes on the Tasman Peninsula
Exploring the colonial history at Port Arthur
Cycling on Maria Island ... home to dramatic landscapes, painted cliffs, wallabies and wombats
A day exploring Freycinet National Park and the stunning wineglass bay
Take in Bicheno's Governor Island Marine Reserve, Whalers Hill, the Blowhole and Fairy Penguin Colony
Bay of Fires and Bridestowe Lavender Farm
The Rugged Northwest highlights include a visit to the famous Tamar Valley Region
A deloraine truffle hunt, mole caves, and platypus viewing
A private visit to Burnie flower farm
A full day excursion exploring the Tarkine Rainforest and Rugged Coastline loop
Cradle Mountain with dove lake hike (weather permitting)
Strahan and a Gordon River Cruise
Lake St Clair walk
Historical Richmond, wineries, artisan providores, colonial history

journeys.name: Wild and Wondrous West Coast
journeys.dates: 12 nights from Feb 05, 2027 OR Feb 16, 2027 | 23 nights from Feb 05, 2027
journeys.summary: True Small-Group Travel: Real connection requires space. To ensure a deeply personal experience and seamless care, we limit our groups to 8 guests, allowing us to offer exceptional personal service and the feeling of travelling with close friends.
includes: Meals as specified (B-L-D), local wine with dinner
All listed activities and entry fees unless marked “optional” (park entry, sculpture drives, Tree Top Walk, winery visits/tastings as specified)
Rottnest Island ferry (Wadjemup) and local bicycle hire where indicated
Surf lesson, truffle hunt, sandboarding and other included activity equipment
Selected tastings and local producer visits noted in the itinerary
journeys.description_html:
Wild & Wondrous West Coast: Coastlines, Karri Forests & Goldfields

This slow‑burn, all‑inclusive small‑group journey for women celebrates nature, culture, and local food & wine. Travel through turquoise bays, karri forests, granite peaks, wild southern coastline, red‑earth goldfields, coastal dunes and limestone pinnacles ... all paced for connection, reflection and shared discovery.
journeys.itinerary_html:
Days 1–2: Fremantle & Rottnest Island

  • Historic harbour town: Wander Fremantle’s foreshore, markets and heritage lanes.

  • Island escape: Ferry to Wadjemup/Rottnest: Cycle turquoise bays, snorkel clear reefs and meet quokkas.

  • Sunset welcome: Relaxed welcome dinner and introductions.

Days 3–6: Margaret River Region: Vineyards & Surf

  • Artistic terroir: Visit Dirt Temple Wine + Art and taste Margaret River’s premium vintages.

  • Coastal wilds: Hike Leeuwin–Naturaliste clifftops and swim secluded beaches.

  • Slow exploration: Cycle through vines, behind‑the‑scenes winery tasting and a group surf lesson.

  • Natural spa: Rejuvenate at Injidup rock pools and explore Yallingup’s creative pockets.

Days 7–8: Pemberton, Karri Forests & Walpole

  • Truffle discovery: Guided truffle hunt in Manjimup with forest tastings.

  • Cathedral trees: Walk among towering karri and tingle forests; Valley of the Giants Tree Top Walk.

  • Coastal vistas: Afternoon shoreline stops and restful evenings in Walpole.

Days 9–12: Denmark → Albany: Coast, Peaks & Culture

  • Pristine swims: Elephant Rocks and Greens Pool in Denmark.

  • Granite ranges: Hike Porongurup and Stirling Range; wine tastings around Mount Barker.

  • Cultural sites: Explore Kalgan River, Aboriginal Fish Traps and Two People’s Bay picnic.

  • Heritage & lookouts: Albany Historic Whaling Station, The Gap and Natural Bridge.

Days 13–16: Fitzgerald River & Esperance Coast (Southwest Edge)

  • Fitzgerald’s biodiversity: Botanic walks and wildflower season highlights; remote coastal lookouts.

  • Pink Lake & pristine sands: Discover Esperance’s Twilight Beach and Cape Le Grand’s white bays.

  • Active options: Climb Frenchman Peak or swim in sheltered turquoise coves.

Days 17–18: Into the Golden Outback: Norseman & Kalgoorlie

  • Landscape shift: Transition from coast to wide open red earth; Norseman stop.

  • Goldrush stories: Kalgoorlie Super Pit viewpoint, Hannans North Tourist Mine and Hannan Street heritage.

Days 19–20: Hyden & Wave Rock Country

  • Iconic geology: Sunset at Wave Rock, Hippo’s Yawn and Mulka’s Cave rock art.

  • Salt lakes & wildlife: Wander salt pans, visit the Hyden Wildlife Park and learn local stories.

Days 21–22: Pinnacles, Sand Dunes & Coastal Play (Cervantes → Lancelin)

  • Heritage detour: Stroll York’s colonial streets and the quirky Tin Horse Highway.

  • Otherworldly landscapes: Late‑afternoon wander at The Pinnacles with sand‑therapy time.

  • Dune adventures: Sandboarding in Lancelin and beach relaxation.

Days 23–24: Perth, Kings Park & Farewell

  • City calm: Kings Park and Botanic Garden vistas over the Swan River.

  • Finale: Final celebratory dinner and smooth transfers to Perth accommodation or airport.

Signature experiences & optional add‑ons

  • All‑inclusive pacing with local hosts, artisan food and regional wines.

  • Optional: Bremer Canyon whale/sea‑life expedition, scenic flights over Wineglass Bay (Freycinet), scuba/snorkel/kayak seal encounters (Port Arthur), and other local adventure upgrades.
journeys.practical_html:
🍽️ Dietary Requirements & Food Culture: Hosted Dining

This trip features premium, guide‑prepared meals and curated local dining experiences that showcase WA’s coastal bounty, Margaret River vintages, wild seafood, native bush flavours and regional produce.

  • Pre‑Trip Notification: Please advise us of allergies, intolerances or strict dietary needs at least 30 days before departure.

  • Tailored Menus: We source directly from local producers and craft menus at our bases; early notice allows seamless substitutions and bespoke dishes.

  • Sea-to-Table Experiences: Many meals centre on seafood and shellfish (Esperance, Albany, Kalbarri/Esperance regions). If you avoid shellfish or seafood, tell us early so we can prepare an equally memorable land‑based local alternative.

  • Bush Tucker & Wine Pairings: Please advise of dietary preferences if you wish to sample native ingredients or participate in tasting-focused vineyard experiences.

🥾 Fitness Level: Active & Adaptable

This journey mixes gentle sightseeing with outdoor exploration across varied terrain.

  • Daily Walking: Expect 3–6 km of on‑foot activity most days on uneven tracks, sand and boardwalks.

  • Key Hikes: Highlights include coastal clifftop walks (Leeuwin–Naturaliste), Frenchman Peak (Cape Le Grand), Porongurup and Stirling Range trails, and short but uneven walks at Wave Rock and The Pinnacles.

  • Optional Challenges: Surf lessons, sandboarding and longer wilderness walks are optional; lower‑impact alternatives are always available.

  • Water Activities: Ability to board small boats/zodiacs, snorkel or partake in optional sea excursions (Bremer Canyon, reef swims). Mild motion and swell possible.

🧳 What to Bring: WA Essentials & Layering

Western Australia’s climate varies — coastal sun, inland heat and cool southern breezes.

  • Sun Protection: High‑SPF sunscreen (reef‑safe for snorkel days), broad‑brim hat, UV‑protective clothing and polarized sunglasses.

  • Layers: Lightweight breathable shirts, a warm mid‑layer for cooler evenings, and a light waterproof for coastal winds or summer storms.

  • Swim & Beach Gear: Swimwear, quick‑dry towel, reef shoes or water sandals for rocky beaches.

  • Footwear: Sturdy trail shoes or hiking boots for uneven tracks, plus comfortable shoes for evenings.

  • Day Pack: 20–30L pack for water, layers, camera and snacks.

  • Extras: Refillable water bottle, insect repellent (for some coastal and bush areas), binoculars, camera and dry bags for electronics.

📋 Practical Details & Logistics

  • Luggage Limits: Due to vehicle space, please limit luggage to one soft‑sided bag (max ~20kg) plus a day pack.

  • Motion Sickness: Optional marine trips (Bremer Canyon, coastal cruises) may encounter swell — bring medication if prone to seasickness.

  • Connectivity & Cash: Mobile coverage is patchy in remote stretches. Telstra generally offers the best rural coverage; bring a small amount of cash for roadside stalls and honesty boxes.

  • Accessibility & Fitness Notes: Many sites feature uneven terrain and limited accessibility; so you should have good balance and stability.

  • Permits & Extra Costs: Optional excursions

  • Travel Insurance: Strongly recommended.
excludes: Personal expenses/drinks beyond those specified (other than wine with dinners)
Optional excursions and upgrades (Bremer Canyon Orca Whale excursion, scenic flights)
Any permits or fees for optional privately arranged activities not listed in the itinerary
journeys.more_details_html:
Western Australia’s south coast and Goldfields are places of scale and story with ancient Indigenous song lines and coastal trading routes, European exploration and gold‑rush towns, and communities shaped by both sea and soil. From Fremantle’s maritime heritage and convict‑era Albany to the pioneering history of Kalgoorlie, the route threads living history through dramatic landscapes: karri forests, granite peaks, windswept bays and the red earth of the outback.

The region’s food culture is intensely local and season‑driven: Margaret River’s world‑class wines and cellar‑door dining; coastal harvests of crayfish, oysters and rock lobster; Esperance’s turquoise‑water seafood; and native bush flavours and artisan producers from truffle farms to lavender fields. Meals are a way into place here ... long lunches, producer visits and fireside cookouts that showcase provenance and craft.

What makes this trip special is its slow‑burn, small‑group intimacy: time to linger in each place, with your expert guides connecting with local hosts to bring stories and connections, and a program that balances wilderness immersion with curated culinary and cultural encounters. It’s a journey of contrasts, sea to forest to outback ... designed for those who want space, sensory richness, and meaningful connection to place and people.
highlights: Key experiences & highlights:
Rottnest Island ferry, cycling, snorkelling and quokka encounters
Margaret River: vineyard tastings, Dirt Temple Wine + Art, Injidup Natural Spa, coastal hikes, group surf lesson
Truffle hunt (Manjimup) and Valley of the Giants Tree Top Walk (Walpole)
Elephant Rocks, Greens Pool and Sculptures Drive (Denmark → Albany)
Porongurup & Stirling Range hikes and Mount Barker winery tastings
Kalgan River walk, Fish Traps and Two People’s Bay picnic
Optional Bremer Canyon Killer Whale expedition (extra cost)
Maria Island cycling, wombat spotting
Freycinet National Park: Wineglass Bay lookout, picnic and swim; optional scenic flight
Fitzgerald River National Park botany and coastal walking
Esperance: Cape Le Grand, Twilight Beach, Frenchman Peak
Goldfields: Norseman, Kalgoorlie Super Pit, Hannans North mine and heritage Hannan Street
Wave Rock, Hippos Yawn, Mulka’s Cave, salt lakes and wildlife in Hyden
Tin Horse Highway, York heritage, The Pinnacles and “sand therapy” at sunset
Lancelin sandboarding and coastal relaxation
Kings Park and final celebratory dinner in Perth

journeys.name: Custom Tours

journeys.summary:
Step out of the noise and into the rainforest, or beyond.
Tell us about your guest profile and dream holiday.
journeys.description_html: We design bespoke itineraries, from intimate getaways for two to celebratory gatherings for small groups. You choose the guest list and we take care of everything.
journeys.itinerary_html: We don’t just plan your perfect itinerary — we travel with you, hosting every moment to ensure the experience is seamless, relaxed, and impactful. Tell us your dates and objectives, and we’ll craft a proposal tailored to your team and budget.
excludes: Optional activities not listed in the itinerary
journeys.more_details_html: From an initial discovery call or email, we map your goals, budget, preferred dates and guest profile, then develop a clear brief and draft itinerary with sample timings, accommodation options, activity choices and pricing. Our planning covers logistics (transport, transfers, on‑site coordination), catering and dietary management.
journeys.small_group_text: Tailored to your circle, with us by your side: Your custom itinerary adapts beautifully to any group size, from an intimate getaway for two to a celebration with your favorite people. You choose the guest list, and we handle the rest. Best of all, we don’t just design the magic, we are right there with you on the road, hosting your journey and ensuring every moment is seamless, relaxed, and unforgettable.

about_blocks.text: We are Barbara and Adele ... friends, collaborators, travel buddies, and hosts. We believe the best travel doesn’t rush, perform, or overwhelm. Instead, it invites you in, slows you down, and leaves you deeply changed.

Once Were Wild Travel was born from our shared love of premium hospitality, outstanding food, fine wine, and wild open spaces. Based in the stunning hills of Maleny where we also welcome guests to our bespoke cottage accommodation, we bring decades of premium hospitality experience to every journey we design.

We create slow, soulful itineraries that balance gentle adventure with exceptional local produce, giving you the space to reflect and connect. Every detail is curated by hand, from the perfect wine paired with dinner to the quiet rest stops along the way.
Barbara (The Spark): The spontaneous spirit who inspires others to try new things, laugh loudly, and embrace the unexpected. Her generous enthusiasm is the heart of our company.

Adele (The Architect): The lifelong host and organizer who meticulously designs our itineraries and logistics. She ensures everything runs seamlessly so you can fully relax, connect, and enjoy the magic.
Join us to rediscover your wild side. Let’s walk a little further, laugh a little louder, and build lasting memories together. We’d love to travel with you.

stories.title: Celebrating a milestone birthday on Kangaroo Island
stories.excerpt: There is a distinct magic that happens when a small group of friends slows down, leaves the mainland behind, and steps into the raw, untamed beauty of Kangaroo Island.
stories.body:
We spent an unforgettable week on this spectacular island sanctuary, gathering together to celebrate a major milestone birthday for Barbara. Kangaroo Island has a way of reminding you what it feels like to be truly connected to nature, and to each other. It was a week filled with laughter, wild encounters, and the kind of soulful luxury we always crave at Once Were Wild Travel. 

With chefs in our group, the culinary highlights were on display daily, but we thoroughly enjoyed a long, lazy "celebratory" lunch at the iconic "The Fig Tree," right across from the shimmering sands of Snelling Beach. This is not your traditional restaurant; it is an immersive dining adventure. We stepped through a secret entrance of tangled branches and fairy lights into a magical outdoor dining room set completely under the canopy of a colossal, 130-year-old fig tree. As dappled summer sunlight danced through the leaves, we were treated to an exquisite, Mediterranean-inspired feast showcasing the very best local, foraged island produce. The only thing more enchanting than the food was the local welcoming committee … wild kangaroos casually roamed the grounds, adding a uniquely Australian touch to our celebration.

Our home for the week was nothing short of spectacular. We stayed at Fides on Kangaroo Beach, a premium, secluded luxury holiday house tucked away on the island's pristine north-western coastline. Elevated right above a completely private beach cove, the lodge offered us breathtaking, uninterrupted ocean views from almost every room. Surrounded by roaming wallabies and coastal wilderness, it felt like our own private paradise. 

But the absolute pinnacle of our week came one quiet afternoon when a pod of wild dolphins swam right into our private cove. They spent hours playing in the crystal-clear shallows right alongside us, making it a surreal, unforgettable birthday gift from nature, that left us all completely breathless. Kangaroo Island completely stole our hearts, proving that you never lose your sense of wonder when you travel with the right people. This island is the epitome of everything we believe in: slow, deliberate travel that leaves you deeply changed. 

We loved this experience so much that we are already planning the ultimate Once Were Wild Travel Kangaroo Island tour.

blog_posts.title: Europe's sweltering heat.
blog_posts.excerpt:
Surviving the European Heatwave: Our Nudist Beach Escape in Slovenia

The summer heatwave sweeping across Europe has been absolutely insane. With temperatures soaring, just walking outside felt like stepping into an oven. We desperately needed a way to cool down, so we took a detour and found the ultimate, uninhibited relief at a stunning nudist beach in Slovenia.
blog_posts.body:
Trading Sweaty Cities for the Adriatic
After days of sticky sightseeing, escaping to the coast was exactly what we needed. Tucked away along the beautiful Adriatic, we stumbled upon a gorgeous, hidden stretch of clothing-optional shoreline Nude Beaches Slovenia.

Stripping away not just the layers, but all the stress and sweat, was incredibly liberating. There is truly nothing quite like diving headfirst into crystal-clear water when you are melting from the summer heat.

Embracing Nature's Cool Down
While this picture does not show you the full-body freedom we experienced, you can see the absolute joy that comes with cooling down from the relentless heat. The vibe at these naturist spots is always so serene and respectful, and it quickly became another highlight of our summer getaway.

If you are ever struggling to survive a sweltering European summer, I highly recommend ditching the heavy clothes and seeking out one of the quiet, natural Freikörperkultur. (free body culture) spots tucked along the coast.

home_faqs.question: How do I book or enquire?
home_faqs.answer: Use the Get In Touch form on our contact page, or email us directly. We reply personally within a day or two and walk you through every step.

home_faqs.question: Can I join as a solo traveller?
home_faqs.answer: Absolutely. Most of our guests arrive solo. Twin share accommodation is the default, and single rooms can be arranged for a supplement on most journeys.

home_faqs.question: Do you have women-only tours?
home_faqs.answer: Yes. Every scheduled departure is curated for women travellers, often aged 45 and beyond, who value depth and connection over crowds and itineraries.

home_faqs.question: How many people are in each group?
home_faqs.answer: Groups are intentionally small, usually six to eight guests, so the pace stays gentle and conversation has room to breathe.

home_faqs.question: What types of tours do you offer?
home_faqs.answer: We design slow, small group journeys for women. Our flagship trips run across Tasmania and Western Australia, and we also host immersive creative retreats in the Maleny hinterland on Queensland's Sunshine Coast.

home_faqs.question: What fitness level is required?
home_faqs.answer: A moderate level of mobility is plenty. We balance gentle walks, rest and beautiful food with the occasional longer hike. If you have any specific concerns, get in touch and we will talk it through.

home_faqs.question: What is included in the tour price?
home_faqs.answer: Most journeys are all inclusive of accommodation, meals as specified, wine with dinner, guided experiences and ground transport. Each tour page lists exactly what is covered so there are no surprises.

home_faqs.question: What happens if I need to cancel?
home_faqs.answer: Cancellation terms vary slightly by journey. Travel insurance is strongly advised for every guest and we will share the exact terms when you register your interest.

home_faqs.question: Do you have insurance
home_faqs.answer: Yes, we have public liability insurance, but you will still need to have your own personal travel insurance. Participating in some activities will require that you are personally insured.

home_sections.heading: Our Approach to Travel
home_sections.body: Hosted personally, paced gently, and curated for women who want their travel to mean something. We work with people, not numbers; with landscapes, not landmarks; and with time, not tick lists. If that sounds like the kind of journey you have been waiting for, you are exactly where you should be.

home_sections.heading: Exploring Tasmania with Once Were Wild
home_sections.body:
Tasmania is a world apart. We cannot wait to welcome you on our all-inclusive Slow and Soulful Tasmania Odyssey, a journey through ancient wilderness, untouched coastlines, unique wildlife, and farm-to-table cellar doors.

Imagine wildlife at dawn, fireside stories at night, and meeting the local characters who make this wild island feel instantly like home.

home_sections.heading: Why Slow Travel?
home_sections.body:
We believe a journey only really begins when the rush stops. Slow travel is not about fewer experiences, it is about deeper ones: longer stays in fewer places, time for unhurried meals, room for conversation, and space to notice where you are.

You will not be moved through this country at the pace of a coach tour. You will walk, listen, taste, rest and return home with stories that actually feel like yours.

home_sections.heading: Women's Small Group Travel in Australia and Beyond
home_sections.body:
Once Were Wild Travel is for women who are quietly ready for something deeper than a tour.

Our journeys move through places that hold special meaning for us, in groups small enough to build genuine friendships, and slow enough to actually arrive. Every itinerary is intentionally shaped around connection: with the raw landscape, with the women beside you, and with the quieter version of yourself that only travel like this can bring forward.
